resolvedQuestions.label: skill auto-loading patterns
resolvedQuestions.summary: No literal skill loader. Knowledge subsystem (.swarm/knowledge.jsonl + hive shared-learnings.jsonl) supplies architect via experimental.chat.messages.transform hook at phase start; coder gets a separate scope-keyed context pack via system-enhancer that calls knowledge_recall on the declared primary file. technicalContext stays an unstructured string. Pattern: orchestrator declares context + hook injects scope-keyed knowledge.

resolvedQuestions.label: stack-agnostic detection
resolvedQuestions.summary: Swarm has explicit detection: src/lang/detector.ts probes marker files (package.json, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, etc.) + extensions; src/lang/profiles.ts encodes per-language commands and prompt constraints; build_check / test_runner / pkg_audit each implement their own ecosystem detection. Static worker prompts STILL leak TypeScript/Bun assumptions — a residual gap. Pattern: detect at tool/hook boundaries, not in worker prompt.

resolvedQuestions.label: caller-restriction enforcement
resolvedQuestions.summary: HARNESS-LEVEL mechanism is the generated OpenCode SDK agent config in src/agents/index.ts:651. Agents named 'architect' or matching name.endsWith('_architect') become mode='primary' with permission.task='allow'; all others become mode='subagent' WITHOUT task permission, so subagents cannot dispatch via the Task tool. Tool-whitelist (AGENT_TOOL_MAP) reinforces. Coder prompt forbids delegation. Suffix classifier overmatches '_architect'/'__architect'/'not_an_architect' (adversarial test asserts these match) — a critical naming caveat. Delegation envelope validation blocks slash_command + invalid targets but is post-authorization.

resolvedQuestions.label: write-capable safety guarantees
resolvedQuestions.summary: Multi-layered, NOT a syscall sandbox. (1) declare_scope tool persists scope to .swarm/scopes/scope-{taskId}.json with versioned payload, validates path traversal/symlinks/plan-presence. (2) scope-guard hook blocks Edit/Write/Patch outside declared scope (WRITE_TOOL_NAMES allowlist). (3) guardrails hook enforces role authority + per-role deny prefixes; declaredScope only relaxes coder allowedPrefix. (4) diff-scope is post-hoc advisory ('SCOPE WARNING', never throws). (5) coder STOP/BLOCKED/NEED recovery protocol explicitly forbids shell/interpreter/redirection bypass. KNOWN GAP: Bash/interpreter writes bypass scope-guard (documented limitation; declare_scope warns about it). MISMATCH: prompt says missing scope blocks all writes, but tests show no-scope falls through scope-guard early-return.

resolvedQuestions.label: sub-agent dispatch contracts
resolvedQuestions.summary: Dispatch is the OpenCode Task tool with structured PROMPT TEXT (TASK/FILE/INPUT/OUTPUT/CONSTRAINT lines), NOT a typed queue or repo-owned worker SDK call. DelegationEnvelope ({taskId, targetAgent, action, commandType, files, acceptanceCriteria, technicalContext, errorStrategy?, platformNotes?}) is the audit-side schema; only commandType ('task'|'slash_command') and errorStrategy ('FAIL_FAST'|'BEST_EFFORT') are typed unions. action is unbounded string. Validation blocks slash_command and unknown targets. NO depth/generation/parentTaskId field — depth bounded entirely by SDK Task permission. Workflow advancement is HOOK-DRIVEN (delegation-gate observes Task tool calls, advances per-task workflow state) NOT response-envelope-driven. Coder returns DONE/BLOCKED text, not a typed result envelope. parallel/dispatcher exists but defaults to no-op (maxConcurrentTasks=1); council parallel dispatch is prompt-instructed.

keyFindings.question: Q3
keyFindings.label: Caller-restriction is enforced in src/agents/index.ts:651 via the OpenCode SDK agent-mode + permission.task config. Architect = primary + task=allow; all others = subagent (no task permission).

keyFindings.question: Q3
keyFindings.label: Adversarial test asserts default workers (coder, reviewer, explorer, sme, critic, test_engineer, docs) lack task permission.

keyFindings.question: Q3
keyFindings.label: Architect classifier is suffix-based (`name === "architect" || name.endsWith("_architect")`) and overmatches '_architect'/'__architect'/'not_an_architect' — naming caveat for our @code agent.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.label: scope-guard hook blocks Edit/Write/Patch outside declared scope (WRITE_TOOL_NAMES allowlist; non-architect sessions only).

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.label: guardrails hook enforces role authority and throws WRITE BLOCKED for unauthorized writes; declaredScope only relaxes coder allowedPrefix step.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.label: declare_scope persists scope to .swarm/scopes/scope-{taskId}.json with versioned payload and validates path traversal/symlinks/plan-presence.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.label: KNOWN GAP: Bash/interpreter writes bypass scope-guard; documented limitation echoed in scope-persistence and declare_scope warning.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.label: Coder STOP/BLOCKED/NEED recovery protocol forbids shell/interpreter/redirection bypass.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.label: Dispatch happens via OpenCode Task tool with prompt text (TASK/FILE/INPUT/OUTPUT/CONSTRAINT block), not a typed worker queue.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.label: DelegationEnvelope schema: taskId/targetAgent/action(string)/commandType/files/acceptanceCriteria/technicalContext + optional errorStrategy/platformNotes; no depth field.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.label: delegation-gate validates envelope and blocks commandType==='slash_command'; observes Task hook to advance workflow state.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.label: parallel dispatcher defaults to no-op (maxConcurrentTasks=1); production code does not import the parallel implementation directly.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.label: Architect-only knowledge injection via experimental.chat.messages.transform hook; isOrchestratorAgent gates non-architects out.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.label: Coder-only context pack: system-enhancer reads declared scope, calls knowledge_recall on first file, injects '## CONTEXT FROM KNOWLEDGE BASE' block — closest analogue to worker auto-loading.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.label: DelegationEnvelope.technicalContext is plain string; no structured skill/capability slot — context is text, retrieval is hook-side.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.label: role-filter preserves system prompts, user delegation envelopes, plan content, and knowledge content from filtering — they are never role-stripped.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.label: Stack detection at the tool layer: detectProjectLanguages scans root + immediate subdirs, matches every profile.build.detectFiles, and falls back to extensions.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.label: Language profiles encode per-language extensions, marker files, and build/test/lint/audit commands — TS/JS/Python/Rust/Go/Java/Kotlin/.NET coverage.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.label: Worker prompts (coder.ts, test-engineer.ts) STILL leak TypeScript/Bun assumptions — residual stack bias even though tools are detector-driven.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.label: system-enhancer hook injects language-specific coder/reviewer/test-engineer blocks from task-file paths via the language profile registry.

ruledOutDirections.label: Looking for env vars or runtime caller-identity checks as the primary caller-restriction mechanism
ruledOutDirections.reason: The mechanism is generated OpenCode SDK agent config (mode + permission.task), not env vars or frontmatter.

ruledOutDirections.label: Treating DelegationEnvelope as a bounded depth/generation contract
ruledOutDirections.reason: No depth/generation/parentTaskId field exists in the envelope; depth is enforced solely by SDK Task permission.

ruledOutDirections.label: Searching for a literal 'skill loader' subsystem in opencode-swarm-main
ruledOutDirections.reason: There is no skill loader; the analogue is the knowledge subsystem with phase-start architect injection + scope-keyed coder context pack.

ruledOutDirections.label: Assuming static worker prompts are stack-neutral
ruledOutDirections.reason: Coder and test-engineer prompts contain TypeScript/Bun specifics; stack-agnosticism lives at the tool/hook layer, not in prompts.
